meta.description: ETF historical monthly price data and computed returns (Reconstructed with realistic market volatility and verified benchmark returns)
meta.data_quality_note: Reviewed and corrected 2026-07-09/10. All 119 ETFs have annual_calendar returns verified against real-world data from official issuer sources (Vanguard/BetaShares/VanEck fact sheets) and financial data providers (Yahoo Finance, FinanceCharts), covering full available history back to each fund's actual inception date. Several funds had incorrect inception dates in the original file (implying years of returns before the fund existed) -- these were corrected. 13 tickers (AGVT, BILL, DBBF, ETHA, GBND, QPON, VBND, VDIF, VEFI, VGSH, VICF, VIF, VMIN) were removed because reliable calendar-year return data could not be located. MONTHLY PRICES: monthly_prices has been regenerated for every ticker so each calendar year's Dec-to-Dec price change reproduces the verified annual_calendar return exactly. Month-to-month movement WITHIN each year is synthetic (randomly generated, category-appropriate volatility) -- only the annual endpoints are real. Do not treat any single month's price as accurate. Stub months before a fund's first full calendar year, and the 2026 partial year (no full-year return exists yet), are entirely unconstrained random walk with no real anchor at all -- these segments are illustrative only. TRAILING / ANNUALISED: an earlier pass computed these from the synthetic monthly price series, which produced wrong results (e.g. QQQ trailing 1y showed as negative when real data shows a large gain) because the synthetic mid-year price path can diverge from where markets actually were, even though the year-end values are correct. This has been fixed: trailing and annualised figures are now computed directly from the verified annual_calendar values only, with no dependency on synthetic monthly prices. IMPORTANT METHODOLOGY NOTE: "trailing" here means the return over the last N *completed calendar years* (e.g. 3y = compounded return of the last 3 full years), NOT a rolling N-year window measured from today's date the way live data providers (e.g. Morningstar, FinanceCharts) report it. The two will differ, sometimes significantly, especially when a strong or weak year sits just inside or outside the window. Sub-year figures (YTD, 1m, 3m, 6m) have been removed entirely rather than estimated, since no real monthly data exists to support them -- populate these only if genuine monthly source data becomes available. VIGH and VGTH (representing funds that only launched March 2026) have empty trailing/annualised/annual_calendar -- zero completed calendar years of real data exist for these two, so no return figures are populated at all rather than showing placeholder/synthetic numbers. MONTHLY PRICE MODEL UPDATED 2026-07-11: switched from independent-monthly-noise to a lower-volatility Brownian-bridge model for the synthetic within-year path. This does not make monthly prices real, but it keeps interior-of-year points much closer to a smooth trend between verified annual endpoints, so arbitrary date-range slices (e.g. a chart tool computing "1 year ago to today" from raw monthly prices, rather than reading returns.trailing) no longer produce wrong-signed or wildly implausible results the way the previous higher-volatility model could (e.g. QQQ briefly showed a -8% trailing year when the real figure was +20.77%). Also fixed a precision bug affecting heavily-decayed funds (e.g. SQQQ, a 3x daily-reset inverse product that realistically loses >99.9% of value over a decade in real life) where a flat /bin/sh.01 price floor was clipping later-year signal; prices now use adaptive decimal precision as they decay. Bottom line: annual_calendar remains the only source of real verified data; monthly_prices is a smoothed illustrative path between those real points, meaningfully improved but still not real month-to-month data. Do not treat any individual month as accurate, even after this update. REMOVED 2026-07-11: all ETFs under au-bond, us-bond, sector, intl, commodity, real-estate, factor, crypto, and leveraged categories (94 tickers) were removed at user request, leaving 25 broad-market equity, au-equity, and diversified-multi-asset ETFs: A200, DHHF, DIA, FAIR, IVV, IWM, MGK, QQQ, SCHA, SCHB, SPY, VAS, VDAL, VDBA, VDCO, VDGR, VDHG, VETH, VHY, VLC, VOO, VSO, VTI, VTV, VUG.

DIA.name: SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust
